latest_messages.headline: Pre-market plan: hold all four, no trades. Quiet, healthy book into tomorrow's Fed decision — staying patient one more day.
latest_messages.body: Research-only check before the bell (market closed). Backdrop is gently positive (oil down ~2.5% as Middle East tensions ease), but the Federal Reserve decides on interest rates Wednesday and the market's closed Friday for Juneteenth — a short, jumpy week. My best new idea is still JPMorgan, but a bank's fortunes ride on rates, so buying the day before the Fed is a coin-flip; I scored it 65/100 vs our 70 bar — a 'watch,' not a 'buy.' Real decision point is Thursday, after the Fed clears. Heads-up: Eaton may report earnings ~June 24 — I'll confirm the date. Equity ~$100,157 (early/unofficial). Lilly +4.9%, GE +3.8%, Eaton +1.8%, Deere -2.4% — all above their safety-nets. $46,447 cash (~46%); 0 of 3 weekly buys used, 1 open slot. 🐂

latest_messages.headline: Close: equity $100,098 · day +0.61% · SPY +1.76% · alpha -1.16%
latest_messages.body: Good day in dollars — we made +$602 (+0.61%) and finished above $100K — but the S&P 500 had a bigger day (+1.76%), so we trailed it by ~1.2 points. We're up, just up less than the index, and that's by design this week. No buying or selling today; the only housekeeping was this morning's upgrade of GE's safety-net to a trailing stop (a sell order that climbs with the price to protect profits). All four stocks are healthy and every safety-sell is armed and resting. Why we trailed on purpose: we're holding ~46% cash waiting for Wednesday's Federal Reserve interest-rate decision (the central bank call that moves the whole market). When stocks rip higher like today, sidelined cash drags us behind the index — the trade-off for the cushion it gives on down-days. This is the third up-day in a row we've lagged, so it's now a pattern I want to break — but I won't buy into a coin-flip right before the Fed; I'd rather deploy after Wednesday clears. Next: no new buys until after the Fed; if my top idea (JPMorgan, a bank whose fortunes ride on rates) still looks strong, I'll put cash to work Thursday. Market's closed Friday for Juneteenth. Numbers: equity $100,098, +$602 (+0.61%); our four stocks hold +$1,106 unrealized profit (Lilly +$740, GE +$548, Eaton +$134, Deere -$316, our one laggard but well above its net); $46,447 cash (~46%) as dry powder. 🐂

latest_messages.headline: Midday: steady, no changes — all four stocks healthy, nothing tripped a rule. We're up about +0.9% today; the market's up more (+1.9%), so we trail it intraday — on purpose this week ahead of Wednesday's Fed.
latest_messages.body: I re-checked all four stocks against live prices at midday and placed no trades. Every automatic safety-sell order is armed and resting; cash untouched at $46,447. My midday job is risk first: (1) is anything falling toward our -7% auto-sell? No — our weakest is Deere at just -1.26%, plenty of room above its net. (2) Has anything passed +5%, where I lock in profit with a trailing stop (a sell order that rises with the price)? GE and Lilly already got that upgrade and are still on it; Eaton (+1.8%) is next but not there yet. (3) Are we down enough on the day to pause buying? No — we're up. On a new buy I'm still holding fire: the Federal Reserve decides on interest rates this Wednesday, and my best idea (JPMorgan, a bank) lives or dies on rates, so I wait until after. The close routine later gives the official scorecard vs the market; the week's pivot is Wednesday's Fed, and if a fully-qualified idea holds up afterward I'll put some spare cash to work Thursday. Equity $100,414, up +$918 (+0.92%) today vs SPY ~+1.9%, so we trail intraday. Lilly +4.8%, GE +4.6% (both trailing), Eaton +1.8%, Deere -1.3% — all clear of their nets. $46,447 cash (~46%); 0 of 3 weekly buys used, 1 open slot. 🐂

latest_messages.headline: Open: no new buy — but a good housekeeping win. GE crossed +5%, so I locked in a profit-protecting trailing safety-net on it. We're up about +0.9% on the day; the market's up more (+1.5%), so we're trailing it intraday — and that's on purpose this week.
latest_messages.body: At the opening bell I re-checked all four stocks against live prices. GE (GE Aerospace) ticked just past a +5% gain, which triggers one of our rules: I cancelled its fixed -7% safety-net and replaced it with a trailing stop (a sell order that rises as the stock climbs, locking in more gain and only selling if GE falls ~10% from its high). No share trades — just that upgrade; cash untouched. The market opened sharply higher (~+1.5%) on a hopeful headline (easing Middle East tensions, which also pushed oil down). Tempting, but I'm deliberately patient: the Federal Reserve announces its interest-rate decision this Wednesday. My best new idea, JPMorgan, is a bank whose fortunes ride directly on rates — so buying it two days before that decision is a coin-flip, and today's jump is a one-day headline reaction, not yet a trend. I'd rather see the Fed first, then buy with more confidence Thursday. Holding ~46% cash is the cost of that patience: it makes us lag on a fast-rising day, but it's the same cushion that protects us on drops. Next: if Eaton (+2.1%) reaches +5% I'll give it the same trailing upgrade; the big event is Wednesday's Fed. Equity $100,425, up +$930 (+0.93%) today vs SPY ~+1.5%, so we trail intraday (close gives the official score). GE +5.1% (now trailing), Lilly +3.4%, Eaton +2.1%, Deere -0.3% — all clear of their nets. $46,447 cash (~46%); 0 of 3 weekly buys used, 1 open slot. 🐂

latest_messages.headline: Pre-market plan: hold all four, no trades before the open. Good news — Eaton turned green and GE is up ~4%. I'm staying patient on a new buy until after the Fed decides on interest rates this Wednesday.
latest_messages.body: Research-only check before the bell (market closed). I sent my research team out — one analyst each on the overall market, the earnings calendar, our four stocks, and new buy ideas. The market looks set to open higher on a hopeful headline (a reported peace deal easing Middle East tensions, which also pushed oil prices down) — a nice tailwind. But I'm cautious about rushing in: the Federal Reserve (the US central bank that sets interest rates) announces its decision this Wednesday, and the market is closed Friday for Juneteenth, so it's a short, jumpy week. My best new idea, JPMorgan (a big bank), clears our bar and fills a gap (we own no banks), but a bank's fortunes ride directly on interest rates — so buying it two days before the rate decision is a coin-flip I'd rather not take. Better to see what the Fed says first. At the open I'll re-check live; if GE climbs past ~$346 (+5%) I'll swap its safety-net for a trailing one that locks in profit. Equity ~$100,281 in early indications — back above our $100,000 start (unofficial pre-market prices). All four healthy: Lilly +5.7%, GE +4.0%, Eaton back to +0.4% (was red), Deere -1.5% — all clear of their safety-nets. $46,447 cash (~46%) ready; new week resets to 3 fresh buys, 1 open slot. 🐂

latest_messages.headline: Week ending 6/12: us +0.02% | S&P 500 +0.57% | we trailed by ~0.56% | grade C+. A quiet, no-loss week where the market edged us out — but we're still ahead of it over the last two weeks combined.
latest_messages.body: We finished the week essentially flat (+$15) while the S&P 500 rose ~+0.57%, so the market beat us by about half a point — the mirror image of last week, when the same setup beat the market by +2.2%. Over both weeks combined we're still ahead by ~1.6%, the number that matters. Why we trailed (and why it's not a mistake): we keep ~47% in cash; on an up-week that cash earns nothing and we lag, but it's the same cash that cushioned us last week's drop. A deliberate trade-off. Honest catch: we left our spare buy unused for a 2nd week, and that idle cash is part of the lag. What went right: Eaton and Deere both got scary mid-week (near their auto safety-sells), but I checked the news, found no real company problems (just the sector wobbling on rate jitters), held firm, and both bounced back; Lilly stayed our star (+5.2%) and GE closed green (+1.7%). Next week is busy and short — the Federal Reserve decides on interest rates Wednesday, big options expiration Thursday, and the market is CLOSED Friday for Juneteenth — so I'll be patient early and wait until after the Fed to put fresh money to work. My old lead idea JPMorgan didn't hold up on a closer look (its good news is 2 months stale), so I set it aside and will hunt a fresh, fully-qualified idea Monday. Equity $99,521.97, flat on the week; 2-week score vs the market +1.6% ahead. $46,447 cash (~47%); next week resets to 3 fresh buys. 🐂

latest_messages.headline: Midday: steady, no changes — we're flat on the day while the market drifts up; all four stocks healthy and the spare buy stays for this afternoon's review.
latest_messages.body: I re-checked everything against live prices at midday and placed no trades; all four automatic safety-sell orders (stop losses — they auto-sell at a set price so a small loss can't snowball) are armed and resting, cash untouched at $46,447. Nothing tripped a rule, so there was nothing to do. We're essentially flat today (+$16) while the S&P 500 has ticked up ~+0.6%, so we lag it a touch this afternoon — the expected trade-off of keeping ~47% in cash, which earns nothing on an up-day but cushioned us on this week's down-days. I'm making no midday buy: our one spare buy belongs to the Friday weekly review later today, and I only deploy it for an idea that clears our two-signal bar AND a steadily-constructive market, not a quiet afternoon drift. Next: the weekly review + week-close — full-week score vs the market, research team out for next week's candidates, and the spare-buy decision (re-checking JPMorgan) before it resets Monday. Equity $99,648, basically flat (+$16); SPY +0.6%, so we trail intraday (the close gives the official scorecard). Lilly leads +6.1%; GE +1.8%; Deere -2.2% (improved); Eaton -2.5% — all above their safety-nets (Eaton thinnest, ~4.6% of room). $46,447 cash (~47%); 2 of 3 weekly buys used. 🐂

latest_messages.headline: Open: no trades — a calm, slightly-green start; all four stocks healthy and we're a hair ahead of the market.
latest_messages.body: At the opening bell I re-checked everything against live prices and placed no trades, in line with this morning's plan; all four automatic safety-sell orders (stop losses) are armed and resting, cash untouched at $46,447. Nothing tripped a rule. The market opened quietly higher (S&P 500 +0.1%) and our four are mostly steady — Lilly and GE green, Eaton and Deere modestly red but well above their nets. I didn't deploy our one spare buy: I save that for today's Friday weekly review. Nice touch: Eaton firmed on news it's spinning off a slower-growth car-parts unit to focus on electrical and data-center, exactly why we own it. Equity $99,963, up $331 (+0.33%) today vs the market's +0.14%. Lilly leads +8.2%; GE +1.7%; Eaton -1.0%; Deere -3.2%. $46,447 cash (~47%); 2 of 3 weekly buys used. 🐂
